sites.title: Bruno Simon
sites.description: A 3D portfolio that turns navigation into play. It is useful design fuel because the interface is inseparable from motion, physics, and personality.

sites.title: Cameron's World
sites.description: A maximal internet collage that treats nostalgia, GIF density, and hyperlink overload as an intentional visual system rather than noise.

sites.title: Cyan Banister
sites.description: Personal site with internet-native attitude, asymmetrical pacing, and a strong anti-template energy that feels more like a live artifact than a portfolio shell.

sites.title: Fox Rockett Studio
sites.description: Design-asset storefront with poster-like product tiles, blunt condensed typography, and a creator-economy commerce rhythm that stays more editorial than SaaS.

sites.title: Neal Fun
sites.description: A playful experiment hub that shows how educational interfaces can feel toy-like, crisp, and culturally current instead of institutional.

sites.title: Savee Marketplace
sites.description: Motion-template marketplace with high-density catalog pacing, direct commercial language, and enough graphic-design culture to be useful for motion-heavy launch surfaces.
